page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with the single-threaded gzip compression benchmark reaching 31.24 GB/sec and the Geekbench single-core score reaching 2134.0, both placing in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation for small language models (135M) at 332.76 tokens/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 15986.8 and large-block memory read bandwidth registering at 124.24 GB/sec.

A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on external Premium Storage. This design optimizes cost efficiency for workloads that do not require high-speed local scratch space, avoiding the premium associated with bundled local NVMe drives. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention.

This hardware profile is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that leverage Intel AMX instructions without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 260 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix math acceleration. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. The server lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage, which offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not need local scratch disks. This configuration is suitable for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core performance and Intel AMX support for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix acceleration, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated CPU-based matrix operations
Strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for single-threaded compression
No bundled local storage, optimizing cost for external Premium Storage configurations
Suitable for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and light inference
